images.caption: Figure 1:Live Music Diffusion Models. Standard block-AR diffusion (top left) concatenates clean context with noisy states over all frames with full bidirectional attention, leaving no way to cache the context encoding despite it staying fixed for each block. LMDMs (right side) route the clean context and noisy target frames through separate projections and utilize custom attention masks to ensure clean context encoding is not impacted by the target frames, enabling KV-Caching over diffusion steps (in Enc-Dec, top-right) or both diffusion steps and or time (in Block-Causal, bottom right). LMDMs enable live interactive musical co-creation on consumer-grade hardware, modulating an array of possible controls in real time (bottom left).

Figure 2:Difference in initial computation graph between standard block-AR diffusion (left) and LMDMs (right, Enc-Dec version). By forcing the initial hidden state to have no mixing between clean and noisy states, and that clean frames cannot attend to noisy ones, we ensure the ability to cache clean frames for efficient inference.

Figure 3:ARC-Forcing.GϕG_{\phi}is post-trained by generating AR rollouts across time with KV Caching, and then passing in the rollouts along with real music (with the same starting context and conditions) into the bidirectionalD​ψD\psi, which uses a relativistic objective.DψD_{\psi}is also trained with an auxiliary contrastive loss on real music with matching vs. mismatched captions to encourage text following.
Figure 4:Global Text-Conditioned metrics over time. In both Enc-Dec and Block-Causal LMDMs, ARC-Forcing strongly reduces error accumulation and the gradual degradation of metrics over time.
Figure 6:Relative CoCoLA score for Accompaniment LMDMs at variabletft_{f}.
Figure 7:User interface of the system built in JUCE leveraged in the user studies and performances.
